goal: Implement v0.3: coverage model + seal snapshot contract — every main commit becomes covered/skipped/uncovered, snapshot contract makes seal evidence precise

summary.title: v0.3: coverage model + seal snapshot contract end-to-end
summary.what: Implements the v0.3 spec across 6 steps. (A) Coverage model in engine/coverage.go with CoverageWindow that classifies each main commit via notes ref + commit message + skip patterns. (B) [mainline.skip] config + Mainline-Skip: trailer with empty-reason rejection. (C) seal --prepare schema bump to v2 with snapshot block (changed_files, worktree_status, evidence_complete, current_branch); seal --submit enforces HEAD/branch/worktree invariants when persisted snapshot exists; --allow-dirty CLI flag is the only escape; IntentSealedEvent gains EvidenceComplete/WorktreeStatus/SealedAtBranch/DirtyFiles for permanent audit. (D) mainline status default Coverage section + new mainline gaps command (text + JSON) with rescue suggestions ordered by reversibility. (E) mainline start --commits/--range backfill primitive with pin_backfill via that bypasses the strategy cascade. (F) AGENTS.md template v5->v6 with v0.3 invariant block + rescue priority section + snapshot contract note. (G) Init now commits all init-created files so fresh repos pass the contract.
summary.why: Two independent dogfood-feedback issues from the mainline-watch user (silently incomplete seal evidence; invisible coverage gaps from manual commits) merge under one strong invariant: every main commit is in exactly one of three states (covered/skipped/uncovered), computed from git facts (notes ref + commit messages), no derived schema. Solving either issue alone would have left the other half. The combined invariant prevents future symptom-chasing API additions and gives the friend MVP user a coherent mental model before handoff.
summary.user_goal: Ship the v0.3 design as production-grade implementation before the friend rolls Mainline out on their project, eliminating the two silent failure modes the dogfood revealed.

decisions.point: Coverage source-of-truth: notes ref vs range overlap
decisions.rationale: Notes follow commits through rebase / cherry-pick automatically; multi-intent shared notes natively supported via intents[]; no derived schema to keep in sync. Range overlap is ambiguous on merge commits and overlapping ranges; cache files create derivation drift.

decisions.chose: CLI flag at every callsite, NO [seal] allow_dirty config
decisions.rationale: Configuration-level safety bypasses become silent failure modes ("oh we set it once, forgot why"). CLI flag forces re-affirmation per submit. The dirty fact still propagates into the sealed event so the audit trail records dirty seals forever.

decisions.point: Snapshot persisted to disk vs embedded in submit input
decisions.rationale: Zero burden on the agent (does not need to round-trip prepare output through their JSON construction); contract is enforced server-side. Re-running prepare overwrites the snapshot intentionally (agent may iterate on goal/turns and re-prepare). Snapshot deleted after successful submit so a stale snapshot cannot ride through a future submit.

decisions.chose: When no snapshot exists, submit proceeds without contract checks
decisions.rationale: Preserves pre-v0.3 behaviour for callers that skip --prepare (test fixtures, automation that constructs SealResult directly). Recommended flow always uses prepare → submit and IS contract-checked. Strict-by-default for the recommended path; legacy-permissive for callers that never opted in.

decisions.chose: Init runs git add + commit for AGENTS.md / CLAUDE.md / .cursor / .windsurfrules / PR template / copilot / .gitignore
decisions.rationale: Without this, fresh-init repos have untracked files from Init itself, which the v0.3 contract would refuse to seal against on the very first attempt. Files Init creates are inherently part of the init commit; bundling them is correct.

decisions.point: Backfill via start --commits, NOT mainline adopt
decisions.chose: start gains --commits and --range flags; reuses the lifecycle
decisions.rationale: Explicit user pushback against new verbs. Backfill is the same lifecycle (start → append → prepare → submit) with different input — which commits to claim. Adding adopt as a separate verb would create a parallel pipeline that drifts from the main flow.

reconcile was just renamed to pin in v0.2 because of semantic murkiness; reusing it would erase the rename benefit. coverage sounds like a config inspection command. gaps is direct and matches the status summary line ("⚠ Uncovered: N").

rejected.reason: Derivation drift risk. Coverage is a direct function of notes ref + commit messages; recomputing on read with cat-file --batch (already shipped) is cheap (status target <200ms).

rejected.alternative: Implement rc6 phase-1 eligibility filter alongside v0.3
rejected.reason: Orthogonal — rc6 fixes which intent pairs phase-1 should compare; v0.3 fixes what counts as sealed coverage. Bundling would slow reviewer feedback on each.

rejected.alternative: Add a Mainline-Skip retroactive note (parallel to commit trailer)
rejected.reason: Two sources of truth (commit message vs note) for the same fact would inevitably drift. Limit skip to the commit message; no retroactive mechanism.
Would let the trailer become a thoughtless rubber stamp. Empty/whitespace-only reasons rejected by SkipReasonFromMessage.

rejected.alternative: Per-actor / per-team config to disable the snapshot contract
rejected.reason: Same time-bomb argument as --allow-dirty config. Per-call CLI flag only.

risks: Init commit list (git add of AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursor, .windsurfrules, etc.) might miss files if a future agent-tool stub is added. Mitigation: explicit list is auditable; new stubs need explicit add. Acceptable risk vs the alternative (`git add .` which would fold in unrelated user work).

Coverage classification depends on commit subject only for skip-pattern matching. A multi-line commit with the trailer mid-body would not be picked up by the trailer path. Implementation extracts the last paragraph (git interpret-trailers semantic) so this matches normal trailer conventions; unconventional placements correctly miss.

Pre-v0.3 sealed intents (no EvidenceComplete field) default to evidence_complete=true / worktree_status=clean in view-rebuild. This is the optimistic default and matches the assumption agents-using-prepare flow always cleaned their worktree. If we discover legacy intents that were actually dirty-sealed, the audit record will read "clean" — not factually correct but indistinguishable from the legacy data we have.

Snapshot file (.ml-cache/drafts/<intent>.prepare.json) accumulates if seal --prepare is run repeatedly without ever submitting. Currently no GC. Cheap (single small JSON per draft) so non-urgent; doctor could surface it later.

Backfill flow: if --commits points at a commit not on main yet (race with merge), Pin will write the note onto a non-main commit. This is permissible per existing reachability filter in scanMainNotes (uncoverable commits are silently dropped on next sync). Documented edge case; agents are advised to backfill only after the target is in main.
followups: Pre-existing PBT flakes (TestPropertyRevertNoteShape, TestPropertyReconcileIdempotent) — second-precision date sort needs topological-order tiebreaker. Identified in PR #29 but split out; unchanged in this PR.
Profile coverage-window status on a 1000+ commit / 100+ intent repo to confirm <200ms target holds; cat-file --batch keeps it cheap but not measured at scale yet.
Auto-suggest skip pattern: when mainline gaps detects same-class subjects (^chore: bump) repeatedly uncovered, prompt to add to [mainline.skip] patterns. Convenience; not v0.3.
Snapshot prepare ref (refs/mainline/prepares/<actor>/<intent>) instead of JSON file: tamper-resistant alternative if needed in v0.4+. JSON-on-disk is sufficient for v0.3.
rc6 phase-1 eligibility filter implementation (4 days; spec already exists, see docs_for_ai/mainline-spec-v0.1-rc6-patch.md).

architectural_claims: Every commit reachable from main is in exactly one of three states: covered/skipped/uncovered. State is computed directly from git facts (refs/notes/mainline/intents + commit message + skip pattern config); no mainline-private derived schema.
Sealed intent claim is precise and tamper-evident: HEAD / branch / worktree-clean checked at submit; worktree status permanently recorded in the sealed event.

Backfill (start --commits) reuses the normal start->append->prepare->submit lifecycle; no parallel adopt pipeline. Pin pins to listed commits explicitly via pin_backfill, bypassing the strategy cascade.
behavioral_changes: mainline status default output gains a Coverage section showing covered/skipped/uncovered counts and uncovered SHAs.
New mainline gaps command (text + JSON) with reversibility-ordered rescue suggestions.
mainline seal --submit fails by default on HEAD drift / branch drift / dirty worktree when a prepare snapshot exists; --allow-dirty CLI flag is the explicit escape.
mainline init now commits AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, .cursor/rules/mainline.md, .windsurfrules, PR template, copilot-instructions.md, and .gitignore in the init commit so fresh-init worktrees are clean.

Sealed event JSON gains evidence_complete / worktree_status / sealed_at_branch / dirty_files / backfill_commits fields. Legacy events read with optimistic defaults (clean / complete / branch=git_branch).

api_changes.signature: IntentSealedEvent: + EvidenceComplete + WorktreeStatus + SealedAtBranch + DirtyFiles + BackfillCommits
mainline status default shows Coverage section
mainline seal --submit gains --allow-dirty flag and HEAD/branch/worktree invariants
